NYCT Female Workforce as of March 31, 2023
NYCT Minority Workforce as of March 31, 2023
NYCT Veteran Workforce as of March 31, 2023
NYCT Workforce with Disabilities as of March 31, 2023

payload.raw_value_text: there were 195 employees identifying as a person with a disability represented less than 1% of the total workforce
NYCT 1st Quarter 2023 workforce increased by 1,003 employees
NYCT handled* a total of 163 EEO complaints
NYCT handled* a total of 31 Title VI complaints
35 EEO complaints were filed citing 83 separate bases, and 4 lawsuits were filed

payload.claim_text: The percentage of females employed in the workforce increased by 1% with a net change of 471 employees when compared to 1st Quarter 2022
The percentage of minorities in the workforce increase by 1% with a net change of 1,336 employees when compared to 1st Quarter 2022

Based on the NYCT utilization analysis, the underrepresentation for females and minorities occurred in the following EEO categories: All Females: Professionals-White, Technicians-White, Protective Service-Hispanics, Asians and White, Service Maintenance-Hispanics and White. Minority Males: Officials & Administrators-Blacks, Service Maintenance-Hispanics.
NYCT will create targeted strategies and initiatives for the following underutilization job groups: Officials & Administrators, Professionals, Technicians, Protective Service, Service Maintenance.

payload.description: PRIDE EXPRESS hosted a Greenwich Village Historical Walking Tour to celebrate Pride Month. Also attended The Hastings Pride Community Festival. Pride Express was instrumental in rollout of MTA Pride Month MetroCard and Pride Decals on trains, subway cars, and trucks.
B.E.G.I.N. hosted a series of events for Juneteenth including a video broadcast, tour of African Burial Ground and National Monument, and members-only networking event at Black owned restaurant.
MTA in collaboration with The Museum of Jewish Heritage held a conversation with Holocaust survivor Maritza Shelley and Marge Ginsburg, MTA Deputy Chief Technology Officer.
Latinos & Friends hosted a Shadow Day providing members opportunity to gain insight into colleagues' roles and responsibilities throughout the MTA.
TransportAsian ERG streamed a two-part documentary celebrating Asian American Pacific Islander Month.
MTA Veterans Employee Resource Group held noontime Memorial Day Observance at 2 Broadway. Guest speaker Patrick Warren, MTA Chief Security Officer and Retired Army Colonel.
Abilities ERG held a Mental Health Awareness Forum with Employee Assistance Program and Work Life Services.

tool_args.label: Patrick Warren, MTA Chief Security Officer

payload.raw_value_text: 3 Title VI and related discrimination complaints were filed citing 3 separate bases
The net change for females was a positive 157 employees
The net change for males was a positive 170 employees
The net change for minorities employees during the 1st Quarter 2023 was a positive 393 employees
The number of employees who self-identified as veterans decreased by 9 employees
The number of employees that self identified as a person with disability increased by one employee
